author: @chuxxsss
text: Make sure you got water on top of that otherwise the quarts will give you silicones.  Sorry for being one of them. Metal beads work get Rob.

author: @StratRider
text:
certainly you are already aware but be EXTRA careful about dust when you open these.
I see you have tops on them but I understand that quartz or certain silica's are extra devastating to the respiratory system.
I have both wet and dry systems for something similar and prefer the wet system due to lead contamination on my items.
Love to see you making your own though as I made my own wet system too.

text:

Suddenly it was clear and made sense! 
I am glad I held my tongue ;0) I do have to say ..... Nice one Rob, nice one indeed!

author: @technosaurus3805
text:
These work pretty well up until the length is greater than 2x the width at which point it seems to work better in the center (near the agitation) than the edges. For large parts it still works ok because it carries the vibration along the axis.

I made one to take the rust off a bumper and then used the same container to nickel plate it, but when I later went to do a batch of parts, the ones on the ends didn't do as well unless I added some rebar that ran almost the length of the container.

author: @user21XXL
text: it could be beneficial to use different sizes of cleaning media at the same time 10mm gravel + sand maybe?

replies.author: @ScarletFlames1
replies.text:
The vibration will just separate them and then you may end up with parts that got cleaned by two different sizes of cleaning media. For example if he put sand+10mm gravel with the wheel hubs what he'll get is a wheel hub that got cleaned at the bottom of it with sand and at the top of it with gravel.

Now there IS a way to get around that problem, and that is to use a denser cleaning medium so it will continuously sink through the more densely packed sand, but now we start getting into the "this is too expensive" and "not the right properties" category of materials. 
Another method will be to pump air through holes at the bottom of the container for a quicksand type of effect. But that would require precision engineering and tougher materials. And I am not sure how this cleaning solution will take to being bubbled through with oxygen.

author: @Nuts-Bolts
text: Wonder if one could make butter in it?

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
Most probably.

I read some time ago that when on westward bound the wagon trains; they would strap a sealed can with mil in it to the wagon where it would get jostled. 
At the end of the day when they stopped for camp; the can would contain butter.
